invariant_definition: As a school-name, Dvaita refers to Madhva's lineage. The school's own preferred self-designation is *Tattvavāda* — the doctrine of (real) tattvas — *not* simply 'dualism.' The position is the strongest realist Vedānta: there is one *svatantra* reality (Viṣṇu) and an irreducible plurality of real *paratantra* realities, eternally distinct from Viṣṇu and from each other (*pañca-bheda*). The user's position-brief and CLAUDE.md explicitly forbid collapsing Madhva to 'dualism' — Dvaita is not Cartesian mind-body dualism, not Manichean good-evil dualism, not even *brahman*-*world* dualism; it is *svatantra-paratantra* asymmetric dependence-realism with *atyanta-bheda*.

per_school.definition: *Tattvavāda*: real distinctions among real tattvas, governed by *svatantra-paratantra* asymmetry and *taratamya* gradation. The five-fold *bheda* (Brahman–jīva, Brahman–matter, jīva–jīva, jīva–matter, matter–matter) is foundational. The *jīva*s are intrinsically classed (*mukti-yogya*, *nitya-saṃsārī*, *tamo-yogya*).

translator_note: Translating Dvaita simply as 'dualism' is the standard scholarly mistake the user's project explicitly forbids. Dvaita is *Tattvavāda* — pluralist dependence-realism with one independent and many dependent realities. 'Dualism' (Cartesian, Manichean, etc.) imposes a binary that Dvaita does not have. Use *Dvaita* or *Tattvavāda* untranslated.
